titleStmt.title: Telegram from Basil Home Thomson, Assistant Commissioner at New Scotland Yard, to Sir Neville Chamberlain, 9 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Assistant Commisioner

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A telegram from the Assistant Commissioner (Basil Home Thomson) to Inspector General Royal Irish Constabulary, Sir Neville Francis Fitzgerald Chamberlain (1856-1944).The Assistant Commissioner is asking Chamberlain to take the necessary steps to monitor any post sent by Patrick Hastings, an inmate of Killadysert Workhouse. The request comes after Hastings sent several letters, described as 'insane' in nature, to the King.Basil Home Thomson (1861-1939) was appointed Assistant Commissioner 'C' (Crime) of London's Metropolitan Police in 1913. He was the head of the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) at New Scotland Yard. When World War I broke out in 1914, the CID found itself acting as the enforcement arm for Britain's military intelligence apparatus: while the newly formed Secret Service Bureau (later known as MI6, the Secret Intelligence Service), and the intelligence arms of the War Office and the Admiralty, collected intelligence on suspected spies in Britain, they had no arrest powers. As head of CID, Thomson was involved in the arrests in several high-profile espionage cases.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6322/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
London R. To Inspector General, Royal Irish Constabulary, Dublin Castle. My telegram April 8th and
your reply patrick Hastings Inmate
Killadysert workhouse county Clare continues
writing insane letters see your
letter 21045 17 th see and
my letter 7 th March 1916
requested that you kindly give
directions to stop annoyance Assistant Commissioner
New Scotland Yard TO (2) I. G. R.I.C. DUBLIN. Castle
